login.title: Railbase admin
login.subtitle: Sign in to continue.
login.noAdmins: No admins yet? Create one with {cmd} or use the bootstrap wizard.
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Quick health overview.
audit.summaryLead: Append-only hash chain — verify integrity with
audit.summaryTail: Every system_admin action is recorded here for forensic review.
audit.filter.sinceTitle: RFC3339 lower bound on the row's `at` column
audit.filter.untilTitle: RFC3339 upper bound on the row's `at` column
audit.exportTitle: Export the current filter slice as XLSX (cap 100k rows)
timeline.summaryLead: Unified timeline — system, admin, and user actions in one append-only journal. Hash-chained; verify integrity with
timeline.summaryTail: Row click opens the full before/after diff.
timeline.empty: No events match the current filters.
timeline.drawer.title: Event details
health.title: Health & metrics
health.subtitle: Live snapshot of runtime, DB pool, jobs, audit, logs, realtime, and backups. Polls every 5 s.
mailer_config.title: Mailer
mailer_config.subtitle: Configure outbound email delivery — SMTP or console driver. Railbase sends welcome notifications and compromise-detection broadcasts when an administrator account is created.
mailer_config.notConfigured: Mailer is not configured yet. Outbound email won't be delivered until you set a driver.
mailer_config.passwordHint: For Gmail-style providers, use an app-specific password.
mailer_config.passwordHintKeep: Leave empty to keep the current password. For Gmail-style providers, use an app-specific password.
mailer_config.probeHint: “Send test email” delivers a small test message here. Doesn't need to be the admin email.
mailer_config.drawerDesc: Pick a delivery driver and fill in the details. “Send test email” probes the values below without saving.
mailer_config.smtpDesc: Production-grade. Point at your provider (Gmail, Mailgun, SendGrid, Postmark, …) or self-hosted SMTP.
mailer_config.consoleDesc: Emails are printed to the Railbase server logs. Useful for local dev.
mailer_config.probeOkTail: Check the recipient inbox (or the Railbase logs for the console driver).
mailer_config.err.probeEmail: Valid email required for the probe
auth_methods.title: Auth methods
auth_methods.subtitle: Choose how end-users sign in. Admin sign-in (this UI) is always password-based and unaffected by these toggles.
auth_methods.drawerDesc: Toggle and configure how end-users sign in. LDAP / SAML changes take effect after a server restart.
auth_methods.restartHint: Config changes take effect after a server restart.
auth_methods.method.passwordHint: Classic email + password sign-in. Recommended baseline.
auth_methods.method.magic_linkHint: Passwordless: emailed one-tap link. Requires the mailer.
auth_methods.method.otpHint: 6-digit code emailed to the user. Useful as a backup factor.
auth_methods.method.totpHint: Authenticator-app codes (Google Authenticator, 1Password, etc.). Optional second factor.
auth_methods.method.webauthnHint: Hardware-key / platform-authenticator sign-in. Optional alternative to passwords.
auth_methods.clientSecretKeep: Client secret (leave blank to keep stored)
auth_methods.ldap.bindPasswordKeep: Service-account password (leave blank to keep stored)
auth_methods.saml.idpInitiatedHint: — opens a CSRF-shaped attack surface; enable only if your threat model accepts it.
auth_methods.saml.spKeyStored: SP Private Key (PEM) — stored; type to rotate
auth_methods.saml.spKeyKeep: Leave blank to keep the stored key.
auth_methods.scim.endpoint: SCIM endpoint URL (paste into your IdP):
backups.title: Backups
backups.col.name: name
backups.empty: No backups yet — click Create backup to make your first one.
backups.help.restoreLead: Restoring TRUNCATES every table in the archive before re-inserting rows. The action is gated by
backups.help.restoreMid: RBAC key, fences user traffic for the transaction window, and records an audit event. CLI alternative:
backups.help.disabledMid: on the server to enable, or use
backups.help.noRbacTail: can restore from the UI; downgraded admins must use the CLI.
backups.help.onDemand: Restore is loaded on demand — the Restore action will appear once capabilities resolve.
backups.restoreDrawer.title: Restore from backup
backups.restoreDrawer.descMid: every table in the archive before re-inserting rows. User traffic to
backups.restoreDrawer.descTail: is fenced for the transaction window. The action is recorded in the audit log.
backups.dryRun.title: Dry-run preview
backups.dryRun.summary: {tables} table(s) — {rows} row(s) will be TRUNCATEd + re-inserted
backups.formatBlocked: This archive's format version is newer than the running binary supports. Upgrade Railbase or recreate the archive on a matching version before restoring.
backups.headMismatch.title: Schema head mismatch.
backups.headMismatch.body: The archive was created against a different migration head. Restoring may leave columns unpopulated or fail outright if the shape diverged. Tick
backups.headMismatch.forceLabel: I understand head heads diverge — force restore
backups.typeToConfirm: To confirm, type the archive name exactly:
backups.typeMismatch: Doesn't match yet — type the filename character-for-character.
backups.sched.title: Scheduled jobs
backups.sched.descLead: Persisted cron schedules. The default destination for
backups.sched.descTail: mirrors the manual archives above — both share the same retention sweep.
backups.sched.empty: No scheduled jobs yet — click + New schedule to add one.
backups.sched.name: name
backups.sched.deleteConfirm: Delete schedule "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
backups.sched.descBuiltin: Builtin schedule — name + kind are locked. You can retune the expression or pause it.
backups.sched.descEdit: Update the cron expression, kind, payload, or pause state.
backups.sched.descNew: Persisted cron job. Defaults to a backup schedule — pick a different kind for cleanup or other handlers.
backups.sched.field.name: Name
backups.sched.field.nameHelp: Letters, digits, underscore, hyphen. Used as the schedule identifier — cannot be changed later.
backups.sched.field.kindCliHint: This kind takes an empty payload. For richer per-kind configuration use the CLI:
backups.sched.field.retentionDaysHelp: Older archives are pruned after a successful backup. 0 disables pruning.
backups.sched.field.outDirHelp: Defaults to <dataDir>/backups so manual + scheduled archives share the same retention sweep.
backups.sched.err.nameInvalid: Name: letters, digits, underscore, hyphen (≤ 80 chars)
collection_editor.newDesc: Define a table and its fields
collection_editor.codeDefined: is a code-defined collection — its schema lives in your app's source and can't be edited from the admin UI. Only collections created here are editable.
collection_editor.nameLockedHelp: The name is fixed once created — rename means drop + recreate.
collection_editor.nameNewHelp: Lowercase letters, digits, underscores. Becomes the table name and the /api/collections/{name} path.
collection_editor.softDeleteHelp: DELETE marks rows as deleted instead of removing them; they stay recoverable via Trash.
collection_editor.deleteConfirm: Delete collection “{name}”? This drops the table and cannot be undone.
collection_editor.col.name: name
collection_editor.placeholder.name: title
collection_editor.err.nameInvalid: Name must be lowercase letters, digits and underscores, starting with a letter or underscore.
collection_editor.err.nameReserved: Names starting with “_” are reserved for system tables.
collection_editor.err.selectNeedsOption: Select field “{name}” needs at least one option.
collection_editor.err.relationNeedsTarge: Relation field “{name}” needs a related collection.
api_tokens.title: API tokens
api_tokens.subtitle: Long-lived bearer credentials for service-to-service auth. Raw values are shown exactly once on create / rotate — copy them then.
api_tokens.col.name: name
api_tokens.revokeConfirm: Revoke "{name}"? Existing clients using this token will lose access immediately.
api_tokens.banner.created: Token created — copy now, it won't be shown again.
api_tokens.banner.rotated: Token rotated — copy now, it won't be shown again.
api_tokens.rotateNotice: The predecessor is still active. Once the successor is deployed, revoke the predecessor explicitly.
api_tokens.field.name: Name
api_tokens.field.scopesHelp: Comma-separated. Advisory in v1 — the token authenticates as the owner with full owner permissions.
api_tokens.placeholder.name: CI deploy bot
api_tokens.createDrawer.title: Create API token
api_tokens.createDrawer.desc: A long-lived bearer credential. The raw value is shown exactly once on create — copy it then.
api_tokens.rotate.title: Rotate token
api_tokens.rotate.desc: The predecessor stays active until you revoke it explicitly. Distribute the successor first, then revoke the old row.
stripe.title: Stripe
stripe.subtitle: Subscriptions and one-time sales. Credentials are stored in the database; the product catalog is authored here and pushed up to Stripe.
stripe.secretKeyHelp: sk_test_… / sk_live_… — mode is derived from the prefix. Leave empty to keep the stored key.
stripe.publishableKeyHelp: pk_test_… / pk_live_…. Safe to expose to browsers.
stripe.webhookSigningSecretHelp: whsec_… — verifies POST /api/stripe/webhook deliveries. Leave empty to keep the stored secret.
stripe.enabledHelp: Master switch. When off, every Stripe call short-circuits — catalog edits stay local.
stripe.credentialsDescTail: Secret fields are kept if left empty.
stripe.noProducts: No products yet. Create one, then add prices — they'll be pushed to Stripe automatically when Stripe is enabled.
stripe.deleteProductConfirm: Delete "{name}" and its prices from Railbase?
stripe.product.name: Name
stripe.product.editDesc: Catalog product — changes push to Stripe on the next catalog push.
stripe.product.newDesc: A catalog product. Add prices to it once created.
stripe.col.name: name
webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: {count} webhook(s). Outbound event subscribers — every matching record event triggers an HTTP POST signed with HMAC-SHA256.
webhooks.col.name: name
webhooks.confirmDelete: Delete webhook "{name}"? Recent delivery history will cascade away too.
webhooks.emptyState.title: No webhooks yet.
webhooks.emptyState.body: Outbound webhooks fan out every record event to your URL. HMAC-signed, retried with exponential backoff via the jobs framework.
webhooks.banner.title: Webhook created — copy the secret now, it won't be shown again.
webhooks.banner.help: Sign incoming payloads with HMAC-SHA256 using this key. See docs/21-webhooks.md for the signature header format.
webhooks.create.title: Create webhook
webhooks.create.description: An outbound subscriber — every matching record event triggers an HMAC-signed HTTP POST.
webhooks.field.name: Name
webhooks.field.events.help: One per line or comma-separated. Dotted patterns; * matches one segment (e.g. record.*.posts).
webhooks.placeholder.description: What this webhook does, who owns it…
webhooks.validation.urlInvalid: must be a valid http(s):// URL
webhooks.validation.eventsRequired: At least one event pattern required
jobs.title: Jobs queue
jobs.description: {count} job(s) total. Showing newest first. Use the {cmd} CLI for cancel / run-now / reset / recover.
cache.title: Cache inspector
cache.description: Live snapshot of registered in-process caches. Polls every 5 s. The Clear button drops every entry AND resets the hit/miss counters for the selected instance.
cache.col.name: Name
cache.confirmClear:
Clear cache "{name}"?

This drops every entry AND resets the hit/miss counters. Active loads in flight will complete; subsequent reads will miss and reload.
cache.empty.title: No caches registered.
cache.empty.body: The cache primitive ships with v1.5.1 but per-subsystem wiring is gradual — instances will appear here as they're registered in {file}.
logApp.summary: {count} event(s) total. Showing newest first. Past 14 days by default (configurable via {setting}). Debug surface — for compliance / forensic review, see the Audit category.
notifications.summary: {delivered} delivered ({unread} unread). Cross-user log of persisted notifications. Showing newest first.
notifications.summaryShort: Cross-user log of persisted notifications. Showing newest first.
notifications.col.title: title
notificationsPrefs.title: Notification preferences
notificationsPrefs.description: Edit per-user notification posture: per-kind/per-channel toggles plus quiet hours and digest mode. Changes are audited as {event}.
notificationsPrefs.empty: No users have notification preferences yet.
notificationsPrefs.drawer.title: Notification preferences
notificationsPrefs.drawer.description: Per-kind channel toggles plus quiet hours and digest mode. Save PUTs the full envelope back.
notificationsPrefs.error.none: No preferences yet for this user.
notificationsPrefs.field.quietStart.help: HH:MM, e.g. 22:00. Blank disables quiet hours.
notificationsPrefs.field.digestHour: Digest hour (0-23, local to digest tz)
notificationsPrefs.field.digestTz.help: Leave blank to inherit the quiet-hours timezone.
notificationsPrefs.preview.label: Send a sample digest to:
notificationsPrefs.preview.disabledOff: Set a digest mode (daily or weekly) to enable preview
notificationsPrefs.preview.enabledTitle: Render and email a sample digest for this user
realtime.title: Realtime monitor
realtime.description: Live snapshot of SSE subscriptions on this replica. Polls every 5 s.
realtime.stat.autoRefreshHint: Polling cadence; subscription state updates without reload.
realtime.error.load: Failed to load realtime stats. Is the realtime broker wired? ({error})
realtime.empty.title: No active subscriptions.
realtime.empty.body: Connect with {cmd} to see one appear here.
sysSessions.title: User sessions
sysSessions.description: {count} session(s) total. Read-only — to revoke a session, use the user record's logout flow.
sysSessions.empty: No user sessions — no users have signed in yet.
sysAdminSessions.title: Admin sessions
sysAdminSessions.description: {count} session(s) total. Read-only — token hashes never leave the server.
sysAdminSessions.empty: No admin sessions — no admins have signed in yet.
emailEvents.col.subject: subject
emailEvents.summary: {count} event(s) total. Showing newest first. One row per recipient per mailer.Send call.
schema.title: Schemas
schema.description: {count} collection(s) registered. Admin-managed collections can be edited here; code-defined ones live in your app's source.
schema.empty: No collections yet. Click “+ New collection” to create one.
schema.col.name: name
settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configuration values that drive the running server.
settings.descriptionLong: Configuration values the running server reads. Click a row to edit; each section maps to a subsystem and unset values fall back to the implicit default.
settings.confirm.reset: Reset "{key}" to its default? The persisted override will be deleted.
settings.reload.restartTitle: Save persists the value, but the running server keeps the boot-time copy. Restart railbase to apply.
settings.reload.liveTitle: Change takes effect immediately. No restart needed.
settings.advanced.summaryEmpty: Advanced (raw key/value editor — no overrides outside the catalog)
settings.advanced.description: Add or edit arbitrary keys persisted in _settings. Catalog-known settings are managed in the typed forms above.
settings.advanced.help: Anything you can persist in _settings as raw JSON. The server stores values as JSONB regardless of the picked type.
settings.advanced.empty: No advanced overrides. Click “+ New key” to add one.
settings.advanced.editor.descEdit: Update the stored value. The key is fixed.
settings.advanced.editor.descNew: A key/value entry persisted in _settings. Catalog-known keys belong in the typed forms above.
settings.advanced.field.keyHelpEdit: The key is fixed — delete + recreate to rename.
settings.advanced.field.keyHelpNew: Lowercase letters, digits, dots, dashes, underscores (must start with a letter).
settings.advanced.field.typeHelp: Server stores everything as JSONB; this picker only coerces the value before submit.
settings.advanced.error.keyFormat: Lowercase letters, digits, dots, dashes, underscores only (must start with a letter).
settings.advanced.error.valueJson: value must be valid JSON (string, number, bool, object, etc.)
records.dataHome.title: Data
records.dataHome.intro: Create a collection to define a table and its fields — it's provisioned in the database immediately and its record API goes live. Code-defined collections (declared in your app's schema) also show up here once registered.
record.form.authCreateBlocked.prefix: Auth collections do not accept generic POST. Use
hooks.title: Hooks
hooks.prompt.newFile: New hook filename (must end in .js; nested paths like sub/foo.js are fine):
hooks.confirm.exists: File "{name}" already exists — open it instead?
hooks.confirm.delete: Delete hook "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
hooks.confirm.discard: Discard unsaved changes and reload from disk?
hooks.empty.title: No file selected.
hooks.empty.line1Prefix: Pick a file from the sidebar, or click
hooks.empty.line1Suffix: to create one. Files are stored on disk in pb_hooks/ and the runtime hot-reloads them within ~1 s of every save.
hooks.unavailable.title: Hooks directory not configured.
hooks.unavailable.line1Mid3: on the CLI) and restart the server. The editor will pick up your
hooks.testPanel.title: Test panel
hooks.testPanel.subtitle: Fire a hook against a synthetic record (no DB writes)
hooks.testPanel.note: Fires the runtime against a synthetic record. No DB side effects.
hooks.testPanel.noRunPrefix: No run yet. Configure the inputs on the left and click
hooks.testPanel.error.recordObject: Must be a valid JSON object
i18nScreen.title: Translations
i18nScreen.descriptionSuffix: Embedded defaults ship in the binary; overrides win when present.
i18nScreen.deleteOverride.title: Remove the on-disk override file
i18nScreen.empty.refEmpty: No translation keys yet. The reference (en) bundle is empty.
i18nScreen.empty.pickLocale: Pick a locale from the dropdown.
i18nScreen.alert.invalidLocale: Invalid locale tag. Expected 2-3 lowercase letters, optionally followed by "-" and 2 uppercase letters (e.g. "en", "pt-BR").
i18nScreen.confirm.deleteOverride: Delete override file for "{locale}"? Embedded fallbacks remain unaffected.
i18nScreen.confirm.openExisting: Override for "{locale}" already exists. Open it instead?
i18nScreen.unavailable.title: i18n overrides directory not configured.
i18nScreen.unavailable.subtitle: Per-locale override bundles for the i18n catalog.
i18nScreen.unavailable.line1Suffix: ) and restart the server. Override files override embedded bundles per-locale; the editor will pick them up on next load.
systemAdmins.title: System admins
systemAdmins.description: {count} admin{plural} total. Read-only. To add or remove admins, use
systemAdmins.empty: No admins — create the first admin via `railbase admin create`.
processLogs.title: Process logs
mailerDeliveries.title: Mailer deliveries
mailerDeliveries.descPart1: Per-recipient delivery state machine — one row per
mailerDeliveries.descPart2: + every provider webhook update (delivered / bounced / complained). Distinct from the Timeline
mailerDeliveries.descPart3: audit event, which records the trigger; this surface records the outcome.
notificationsLog.title: Notifications log
notificationsLog.description: Cross-user log of persisted in-app notifications. Distinct from the Notifications preferences editor (Settings → Notifications) which controls per-user delivery preferences.
forgotPassword.title: Reset password
forgotPassword.subtitle: We'll send a one-time reset link to your admin email.
forgotPassword.sentPart2: is registered, a password-reset link has been sent. Check your inbox (and spam folder).
forgotPassword.sentValidity: The link is valid for 1 hour and can only be used once.
forgotPassword.mailerDownDesc: Your server can't send email yet, so the link can't reach you. Reset the password from a shell with operator access:
forgotPassword.mailerDownFollowup: After resetting, sign in with the new password and configure the mailer from
passwordReset.title: Set a new password
passwordReset.subtitle: Pick a strong password. This link can only be used once.
passwordReset.tokenMissingSubtitle: This page expects a reset token in the URL.
passwordReset.tokenMissingBody: No reset token in the URL. Reset links arrive by email — open the link in that email, or request a new one.
passwordReset.successRedirect: Password updated. Redirecting to sign in…
mailerTpl.title: Mailer templates
mailerTpl.description: Read-only viewer for the built-in email templates plus operator overrides.
mailerTpl.helpPart1: Read-only viewer. To override a built-in, write the markdown to
mailerTpl.helpPart2: ; the Mailer picks it up on next send (or after restart pending v1.0.1 hot-reload).
mailerTpl.pickKindHint: Pick a template kind from the left to view its current content.
mailerTpl.noOverride: No override on disk — Mailer renders the embedded built-in.
trash.title: Trash
trash.descPart1: Soft-deleted records across all collections with
trash.descPart2: Records here can be restored or stay until your retention policy permanently purges them.
trash.emptyHint: Soft-deleted records linger here instead of being physically removed, so an accidental delete is one click away from being undone.
trash.noSoftDeletePart2: enabled. Add the flag to a collection in your schema to start collecting tombstones here.
admins.title: Admins & roles
admins.description: Assign or downgrade an admin's site role-set. The backend refuses to leave the deployment with zero system_admins.
admins.empty.none: No admins yet — create one via `railbase admin create` or the bootstrap wizard.
admins.editor.title: Edit roles
admins.editor.warnZero: Warning: an admin with zero site roles can sign in but every rbac-gated handler will deny them.
admins.inspector.bypassDesc: Grants every action. Holders are not denied any rbac-gated handler.
admins.inspector.noActions: No actions granted. Holders are denied every rbac-gated handler.
bootstrap.welcomeDescPart1: Choose where to store your data. You can change this later by editing
bootstrap.configured.part1: Database is already configured — running against your external PostgreSQL. You can re-run the wizard to change targets, or skip straight to
bootstrap.driver.embedded.title: Use embedded PostgreSQL
bootstrap.driver.embedded.subtitle: Bundled Postgres for local development — already running, no configuration needed
bootstrap.driver.localSocket.title: Use my local PostgreSQL
bootstrap.driver.localSocket.notDetected: No local PostgreSQL detected on this machine
bootstrap.driver.external.title: Use an external PostgreSQL
bootstrap.driver.external.subtitle: Managed Postgres (Supabase, Neon, RDS, …) or a remote host
bootstrap.usernamePlaceholder: Postgres role (often your OS user — not an email)
bootstrap.passwordHelp: Leave empty for peer/trust auth (local sockets often don't need a password).
bootstrap.createDbIfMissing: Create the database if it doesn't exist
bootstrap.saveLockedTitle: Confirm the warning above before saving.
bootstrap.reloadingDesc: The server is applying migrations and restarting in-place. This page will refresh automatically once it's ready — no terminal commands needed.
bootstrap.restartDesc: The configuration is saved. Re-run the process to pick up the new DSN.
bootstrap.foreignDb.title: This database is not empty.
bootstrap.foreignDb.foundPart3: schema, but none of them is a Railbase marker. Railbase expects either an empty database or an existing Railbase instance — saving now would install service tables and Postgres extensions alongside another app's data.
bootstrap.foreignDb.intentional: If this is intentional (e.g. you're co-locating Railbase with another app in the same DB), tick the box to confirm.
bootstrap.foreignDb.confirm: I understand — install Railbase alongside the existing tables.
bootstrap.existing.title: Existing Railbase instance detected.
bootstrap.existing.descPart2: marker — this database already belongs to Railbase. Saving will reconnect the running process to it and apply any pending migrations.
bootstrap.probe.willCreate: Database will be created on save.
bootstrap.save.afterRestart: After saving, restart railbase. The next boot will use your PostgreSQL instead of embedded.
bootstrap.adminStep.title: Create the first admin
bootstrap.adminStep.passwordHelp: Minimum 8 characters. Use the dice to generate a strong one.
